intent: Embed every stable section_id as a reusable machine object. Prefer the existing sbert-embedder station / Infinity embedding service when reachable; if the service is unavailable, emit deterministic hash-based fallback vectors and mark provenance.mocked = true instead of blocking the workflow.

input.format: section-map.json supplies {paper_uuid, page_id?, sections:[{section_id, stable_uuid, heading_path, ordinal, packet_path, text_excerpt, word_count}]}. metadata.json supplies {page_id, title, source_file_name, address_string}. If upstream packets are missing, lane falls back to section text excerpts or the raw 00_DROP file.
input.mock_policy: If 02 is missing, lane synthesizes a minimal section ledger from the raw drop file. If the SBERT/Infinity service is unavailable, lane emits deterministic hash vectors with provenance.mocked = true and vector_source = 'hash-fallback'.

output.format: section-vectors.jsonl is one JSON object per section with embedding payload, metadata, and provenance. vector-metadata.json is the page-level rollup including source mode, vector dimension, counts, nearest-neighbor hints, and loopback state.

vector_policy.normalization: L2 normalize all embeddings before writing artifacts.
vector_policy.fap_overlap: If an upstream FAP packet already contains section-level embeddings for the exact page_id + section_id set, consume those instead of re-embedding. Record source as 'fap-upstream'.

loopback_conditions: Section count in output differs from 02_SECTION_MAP section_count.
Any section resolves to empty text after packet/text-excerpt fallback.
Embedding service returns inconsistent dimensions across one run.
All section vectors are numerically identical for a multi-section document.
Synthetic upstream section map had to be built because 02 output was absent.

models: hash fallback only when station path is unavailable
